Mar 6, 2006 PERL on the Instructional UNIX Systems -------------------------------------- Perl is available on the Instructional UNIX Systems in /usr/sww/bin/perl which is a link to the current version (type "perl -V" to display the

the current version (type "perl -V" to display the version and default @INC path). Documentation can be found with the command man perl See /share/b/pub/software.help for more information about the "man" command. (Feb 2003) Perl 5.8.0 i

bout the "man" command. (Feb 2003) Perl 5.8.0 is the default version in on the Software Warehouse, for Solaris 9 (X86 and Sparc) Perl 5.8.0 is also available. Shared files: /usr/sww/pkg/perl-5.8.0/lib Executable: /usr/sww/pkg/perl-5.8.0/bin

5.8.0/lib Executable: /usr/sww/pkg/perl-5.8.0/bin Documentation: /usr/sww/pkg/perl-5.8.0/man Archive URL: https://iris.eecs.berkeley.edu/idsg/sww/software/ PERL on the Instructional NT Systems ------------------------------------ Perl is not c

---------------------------------- Perl is not commonly installed on the local diks of the Instructional NT systems. It is available on a shared disk, \\ntsww\newsww\perl . See /share/b/pub/microsoft.help for information about connecting a s
icrosoft.help for information about connecting a shared disk to a local Win2K workstation. Instructional Support Group 378/386/384 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
